title: BetterInvesting™ Magazine Update on Sonos and United Rentals
text: TROY, Mich. , May 23, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- The Editorial Advisory and Securities Review Committee of BetterInvesting Magazine today announced Sonos Inc. (NASDAQ: SONO) as its "Stock to Study" and United Rentals Inc. (NYSE: URI) as its "Undervalued Stock" in the August 2022 issue for investors' informational and educational use.

title: 3 Top Stocks Primed to Take Off in a Rising Rate Environment
text: For investors looking for ways to battle this high inflation and rising rate environment, here are three top stocks to add to your watchlist. The post 3 Top Stocks Primed to Take Off in a Rising Rate Environment appeared first on InvestorPlace.

title: Bargain Hunters Will Love United Rentals
text: United Rentals is the world's largest equipment rental company with a great track record of outperforming in a fragmented industry. Despite aggressive M&A, the company has a healthy balance sheet, which means that most of its free cash flow ends up boosting the share price via buybacks.

title: United Rentals (URI) to Buy Ford Pro's All-Electric Vehicles
text: United Rentals' (URI) investment in Ford Pro's 100% electric vehicles to support its commitment to provide alternative energy solutions for customers and operations.

title: Ford stock rises after EV buying agreement with United Rentals
text: Shares of Ford Motor Co. F, -5.91% rose 1.5% in premarket trading Tuesday, after the automaker and equipment rental company United Rentals Inc. URI, -2.92% announced an agreement in which United Rentals to buy electric vehicles from Ford. United Rentals stock were indicated up more than 1.5%.

title: United Rentals Invests in Ford Electric Trucks for North American Fleet
text: STAMFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--United Rentals, Inc. (NYSE: URI), the world's largest equipment rental company, today announced a new agreement with Ford Pro to purchase all-electric vehicles for its North American rental and company fleets. The agreement initially includes orders for 500 F-150 Lightning trucks and 30 E-Transit vans, with 120 trucks and all 30 vans to be delivered in 2022.
